titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Faculty of Engineering, Yale University, Records
Faculty of Engineering, Yale University, Records
titlestmt.author: compiled by Daniel Hartwig

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday September 3, 2020 at 09:07

did.unittitle: Faculty of Engineering, Yale University, records
did.abstract: The records consist of administrative files documenting the academic and professional activities of the Faculty of Engineering at Yale.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Forms part of Yale Record Group 14-B (YRG 14-B), Records of academic departments and programs of the Yale Faculty of Arts and Sciences.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Access to the records is restricted. See Collection Contents for details.
Some records in this finding aid have been redacted, as they include student names, donor names, and other restricted data. These records will not appear in the published finding aid.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Faculty of Engineering, Yale University, Records (RU 200). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The records are arranged by subject.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The records consist of administrative files documenting the academic and professional activities of the Faculty of Engineering at Yale.

did.unittitle: Yale University. Faculty of Arts and Sciences. Records of Faculty of Engineering, 1986-1994
Engineering and Applied Sciences department, undergraduate courses

Mechanical engineering, space shuttle experiments (NASA), Professor Apfel
Engineering, "twilight" graduate courses, Charles K. Kao
Engineering, electrical, Hewlett-Packard and Thomson Components, Mostek, Tom Cunningham
Engineering, electrical; Morse teaching center, dedication and reception

Engineering, Microelectronics center, Microfabrication facility, Nanometrics gift

Engineering and Applied Science, first reunion
Applied Science, Gyrotron, Jay L. Hirschfield
Engineering, council of receptions, faculty, staff, graduate students

Engineering, council of; reception, faculty and staff

Engineering, chemical; Professor Gary L. Haller

Posters made spring 1971 for EDS newsletter
